title: Diogenes Laertius: Life of Epicurus
description: From Diogenes Laertius, Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers — our main source for the lives of ancient philosophers.
introduction: From Diogenes Laertius, Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers — our main source for the lives of ancient philosophers.

translation.text: Epicurus taught that pleasure is the highest good. But by pleasure he did not mean sensual indulgence, but the absence of pain in body and trouble in soul. He died with calm philosophical conversation despite agonizing pain.
